description_wiki: The September 11 attacks, colloquially known as 9/11, were a coordinated series of suicide attacks perpetrated by the Islamist terrorist organization al-Qaeda against the United States in 2001.

description: former skyscraper complex in Manhattan, New York (1968-2001)
description_wiki: The original World Trade Center (WTC) was a complex of seven buildings in the Financial District of Lower Manhattan in New York City.

aliases: Headquarters of the United States Department of Defense
Headquarters of the United States Department of War (2025–present)
Headquarters of the United States Department of Defense (1947–2025)
Headquarters of the United States Department of War (1789–1947)
description: headquarters of the United States Department of Defense; named after the shape of the building, a polygon with five (greek: penta) sides
description_wiki: The Pentagon is the headquarters of the United States Department of Defense, in Arlington County, Virginia, across the Potomac River from Washington, D.C. The building was constructed on an accelerated schedule during World War II. As a symbol of the U.S. military, the phrase The Pentagon is often used as a metonym for the Department of Defense and its leadership.

description: national cemetery in Virginia, United States
description_wiki: Arlington National Cemetery is the largest cemetery in the United States National Cemetery System, one of two maintained by the United States Army.

description: 7,142-acre parkway in Virginia and the District of Columbia in the United States, that is maintained by the National Park Service
description_wiki: The George Washington Memorial Parkway, colloquially the G.W. Parkway, is a 25-mile-long (40 km) limited-access parkway that runs along the south bank of the Potomac River from Mount Vernon, northwest to McLean, Virginia, United States, and is maintained by the National Park Service (NPS).

description: park in Fairfax County, Virginia, United States of America

description_wiki: The National Mall is a landscaped park near the downtown area of Washington, D.C., the capital city of the United States.

description: memorial in Washington, D.C., United States
description_wiki: The Thomas Jefferson Memorial is a national memorial in Washington, D.C., built in honor of Thomas Jefferson, the principal author of the United States Declaration of Independence and the nation's third president.

description: group of museums and research centers administered by the United States government
description_wiki: The Smithsonian Institution ( smith-SOH-nee-ən; or simply the Smithsonian) is a group of museums, education and research centers, created by the United States federal government "for the increase and diffusion of knowledge".

surface_forms: Commander for the Central District of the park police
description_wiki: The United States Park Police (USPP) is the oldest uniformed federal law enforcement agency in the United States.

description: combined military forces of the United States
description_wiki: The United States Armed Forces are the military forces of the United States.

description: city in Pierce County, Washington, United States
description_wiki: Lakewood is a city in Pierce County, Washington, United States.

description: city in and county seat of King County, State of Washington, United States
description_wiki: Seattle (  see-AT-əl) is the most populous city in the U.S. state of Washington and the Pacific Northwest region of North America.

description: national recreation area in the U.S. state of Washington
description_wiki: Lake Roosevelt National Recreation Area is a U.S. national recreation area that encompasses the 130-mile (210 km) long Franklin D. Roosevelt Lake between Grand Coulee Dam and Northport, Washington, in eastern Washington state.
